texto: I like to play the grey areas in life - that's the most uncomfortable place to be. Nobody likes to be in that in-between state where there don't know what's going to happen. There's a lot of tension in that, and a lot of stuff to play with - where it's uncomfortable and awkward and sad and scary.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Melanie Lynskey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I like to play the grey areas in life - that's the most uncomfortable place to be. Nobody likes t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, know, play pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de know e play.
